title: Postmark Documentation
prompt_source: Inline docs prompt; CONNECTOR_DOCUMENTATION_GUIDELINES fetched via getDocGuidelines()

sections.title: Overview
sections.content:
**Postmark** is a transactional + broadcast email delivery platform exposing a REST API at `https://api.postmarkapp.com`. This connector — `PostmarkConnector` (`CONNECTOR_TYPE = "postmark"`, `AUTH_TYPE = "api_key"`) — wraps the operational surfaces a Shielva tenant typically needs from a Postmark account:

| Surface | Base path | Token kind | Capability |
|---|---|---|---|
| Email send | `/email`, `/email/batch`, `/email/withTemplate` | server | Single send, batch send (≤500), templated send |
| Server info | `/server` | server | Server settings + health probe |
| Outbound messages | `/messages/outbound` | server | Paginated search + per-message detail |
| Inbound messages | `/messages/inbound` | server | Inbound message stream listing |
| Bounces | `/bounces`, `/bounces/{id}`, `/bounces/{id}/activate` | server | List, fetch, reactivate suppressed recipient |
| Templates | `/templates`, `/templates/{id_or_alias}` | server | Catalogue + content fetch + create |
| Stats | `/stats/outbound` | server | Aggregated outbound delivery stats |
| Servers | `/servers`, `/servers/{id}` | **account** | Account-wide server registry |
| Domains | `/domains` | **account** | Sender-domain registry + DKIM/SPF status |

The connector normalises outbound messages into `NormalizedDocument` (id = `f"{tenant_id}_{source_id}"`), surfaces standalone `async def` methods per user-requested operation (OCP), retries 429/5xx with exponential backoff (3 attempts), and never embeds raw HTTP in `connector.py` (SOC — all HTTP delegated to `client/http_client.py::PostmarkHTTPClient`).

A key Postmark gotcha: the platform has **two distinct credentials** that are NOT interchangeable. The connector handles the split internally — see *Authentication* below.

sections.title: Quick Start
sections.content:
### 1. Generate Postmark tokens

1. Sign in to https://account.postmarkapp.com.
2. **Server token** (required) — open **Servers → your sending server → API Tokens → Add API Token**. Copy the value; Postmark only shows it once.
3. **Account token** (optional) — open **Account (top-right) → API Tokens → Add API Token**. Only needed if you'll call `/servers` or `/domains`.
4. **Verify a sender domain** — **Sender Signatures / Domains** → add your domain → publish the DKIM CNAME + Return-Path TXT records. Postmark refuses sends from unverified domains.

### 2. Install in ACP

In **ACP → Manage Connectors → Postmark**, fill in:

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| `server_token` (secret, required) | Server API token from step 2 |
| `account_token` (secret, optional) | Account API token from step 3 — only if you need `list_servers` / `list_domains` |
| `default_from_email` (text, optional) | Verified sender, e.g. `no-reply@yourdomain.com` |
| `base_url` (text, optional) | Override `https://api.postmarkapp.com` (rarely needed) |
| `rate_limit_per_min` (number, default 600) | Client-side soft cap |

Click **Install**. `install()` validates `server_token` is present, then probes `GET /server` to confirm the token is real. 401 → `MISSING_CREDENTIALS`. Network error → `DEGRADED but installed`.

### 3. First send

```python
result = await connector.send_email(
    from_email="no-reply@yourdomain.com",
    to="user@example.com",
    subject="Welcome",
    text_body="Thanks for signing up.",
    tag="welcome",
)
# {"MessageID": "...", "SubmittedAt": "...", "ErrorCode": 0, "Message": "OK"}
```

Inactive recipients raise `PostmarkInactiveRecipient` so you can deactivate or re-route without re-parsing JSON.

sections.title: Authentication
sections.content:
Postmark REST API uses **server-to-server API token authentication** (`AUTH_TYPE = "api_key"`). No OAuth dance, no token refresh, no expiry.

### Two credentials — server vs account

Postmark draws a hard line between **server-scoped** and **account-scoped** APIs:

| Credential | Header | Scope | Used by |
|---|---|---|---|
| `server_token` | `X-Postmark-Server-Token` | One sending server | `/email/*`, `/server`, `/messages/*`, `/bounces`, `/templates`, `/stats/*` |
| `account_token` | `X-Postmark-Account-Token` | Whole Postmark account | `/servers`, `/domains`, `/senders` |

They are **not interchangeable** — sending the account token to `/email` is a 401, and vice-versa. The connector takes `server_token` as **required** and `account_token` as **optional**; `client/http_client.py::_headers(endpoint_kind=…)` picks the right header per call so the wrong credential is never sent on the wire.

Calling an account-scoped method (`list_servers` / `list_domains` / `get_server_by_id`) without `account_token` configured raises `PostmarkAuthError` **immediately** — no 401 round-trip required.

### Lifecycle

| Phase | Behaviour |
|---|---|
| `install()` | Validates `server_token` non-empty; probes `GET /server`. 401 → `MISSING_CREDENTIALS`. Network error → `DEGRADED` but installed. |
| `authorize(...)` | No-op for api_key auth — returns `TokenInfo(token_type="api_key", access_token=server_token)` for ABI compatibility. |
| `health_check()` | `GET /server` as a lightweight probe. |
| `ensure_token()` | N/A — no token lifecycle. |
| token storage | None — the tokens live in connector config. |

### Header contract

Every server-scoped outbound request:

```
X-Postmark-Server-Token: <server_token>
Accept:                  application/json
Content-Type:            application/json
```

Every account-scoped outbound request:

```
X-Postmark-Account-Token: <account_token>
Accept:                   application/json
Content-Type:             application/json
```

sections.title: Configuration
sections.content:
### Install fields (per-tenant)

Read via `self.config.get(...)` in `connector.py::__init__` — never hardcoded.

| Key | Type | Required | Read in code | Purpose |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| `server_token` | secret | yes | `self.server_token` | `X-Postmark-Server-Token` header value |
| `account_token` | secret | no | `self.account_token` | `X-Postmark-Account-Token` header value (only for `/servers`, `/domains`) |
| `default_from_email` | string | no | `self.default_from_email` | Used when caller omits `from_email` on send |
| `base_url` | string | no | `self.base_url` (default `https://api.postmarkapp.com`) | Override for sandbox or proxy |
| `rate_limit_per_min` | number | no | `self.rate_limit_per_min` (default 600) | Client-side soft cap |

### Class constants (provider-wide)

```python
REQUIRED_CONFIG_KEYS = ["server_token"]
_STATUS_MAP = {
    401: ("OFFLINE",   "TOKEN_EXPIRED"),
    403: ("UNHEALTHY", "INVALID_CREDENTIALS"),
    429: ("DEGRADED",  "CONNECTED"),
}
_POSTMARK_BASE = "https://api.postmarkapp.com"
```

Note: `account_token` is intentionally **not** in `REQUIRED_CONFIG_KEYS`. Callers that try to use an account-scoped method without it get an immediate `PostmarkAuthError`; everything else works on `server_token` alone.

sections.title: API Methods
sections.content: All public async methods on `PostmarkConnector`. SOC: `connector.py` orchestrates only; HTTP lives in `client/http_client.py::PostmarkHTTPClient`.

children.title: Lifecycle
children.content:
### `async install() -> ConnectorStatus`
Validate `server_token`; probe `GET /server`. Returns `HEALTHY+CONNECTED` on 2xx, `OFFLINE+MISSING_CREDENTIALS` on 401, `DEGRADED+CONNECTED` on transport failure.

### `async authorize(auth_code, state) -> TokenInfo`
No-op for api_key auth — returns a `TokenInfo` whose `access_token` is the configured `server_token`.

### `async health_check() -> ConnectorStatus`
`GET /server`. Classifies failures by `_STATUS_MAP`.

### `async sync(since=None, full=False, kb_id=None, webhook_url=None) -> SyncResult`
Iterates the first 100 outbound messages, fetches per-message details, normalizes each into a `NormalizedDocument`, and calls `ingest_document` per message. Stores `last_outbound_offset` checkpoint.

children.title: Email Send
children.content:
### `async send_email(from_email, to, subject, *, html_body=None, text_body=None, cc=None, bcc=None, tag=None, metadata=None, message_stream="outbound") -> Dict`
`POST /email`. Requires at least one of `html_body`/`text_body` — surfaced as `ValueError` instead of a 422 round-trip. Inactive recipients raise `PostmarkInactiveRecipient`.

### `async send_email_batch(messages: List[Dict]) -> List[Dict]`
`POST /email/batch` — send up to 500 messages per call. The list is sent as the raw JSON body (not wrapped).

### `async send_email_with_template(template_id=None, template_alias=None, from_email=None, to=None, template_model=None, message_stream="outbound") -> Dict`
`POST /email/withTemplate`. Exactly one of `template_id` or `template_alias` must be supplied — surfaced as `ValueError` instead of a 422.

children.title: Messages (Outbound + Inbound)
children.content:
### `async list_messages(count=50, offset=0, *, recipient=None, from_email=None, tag=None, status=None) -> Dict`
`GET /messages/outbound` — paginated search.

### `async get_message_details(message_id) -> Dict`
`GET /messages/outbound/{id}/details` — full envelope + delivery events.

### `async get_message(message_id) -> NormalizedDocument`
Convenience wrapper — fetches details and runs them through `normalize_message`.

### `async list_inbound_messages(count=50, offset=0, *, recipient=None, from_email=None, subject=None, status=None) -> Dict`
`GET /messages/inbound` — paginated inbound search.

children.title: Bounces
children.content:
### `async list_bounces(count=50, offset=0, *, type=None, inactive=None, email_filter=None) -> Dict`
`GET /bounces` — paginated bounce log.

### `async get_bounce(bounce_id) -> Dict`
`GET /bounces/{id}`.

### `async activate_bounce(bounce_id) -> Dict`
`PUT /bounces/{id}/activate` — reactivates a previously suppressed recipient.

children.title: Templates
children.content:
### `async list_templates(count=50, offset=0) -> Dict`
`GET /templates` — paginated catalogue.

### `async get_template(template_id_or_alias) -> Dict`
`GET /templates/{id_or_alias}` — fetch template content + HTML/text bodies.

### `async create_template(name, subject, *, html_body=None, text_body=None, alias=None) -> Dict`
`POST /templates`. Requires at least one of `html_body`/`text_body`.

children.title: Stats
children.content:
### `async get_stats_overview(tag=None, from_date=None, to_date=None) -> Dict`
`GET /stats/outbound` — aggregated delivery stats. Date params accept Postmark's `YYYY-MM-DD` format.

children.title: Account-scoped (require account_token)
children.content:
These methods raise `PostmarkAuthError` immediately when `account_token` is not configured — no 401 round-trip.

### `async list_servers(count=50, offset=0) -> Dict`
`GET /servers` — account-wide server registry.

### `async get_server_by_id(server_id) -> Dict`
`GET /servers/{id}` — per-server detail.

### `async list_domains(count=50, offset=0) -> Dict`
`GET /domains` — sender-domain registry with DKIM/SPF status.

sections.title: Error Handling
sections.content:
Exception hierarchy in `exceptions.py`:

```
PostmarkError                         # base; carries status_code + response_body
├── PostmarkAuthError                 # 401 / 403 / ErrorCode 10
├── PostmarkBadRequestError           # 400 / 422 (non-special ErrorCode)
├── PostmarkNotFoundError             # 404
├── PostmarkConflictError             # 409
├── PostmarkPreconditionError         # 428
├── PostmarkRateLimitError            # 429 — retry_after_s
├── PostmarkServerError               # 5xx
└── PostmarkInactiveRecipient         # 422 with ErrorCode 406
```

Back-compat aliases preserved:
```
PostmarkNetworkError = PostmarkServerError   # legacy name
PostmarkNotFound     = PostmarkNotFoundError
```

### Postmark ErrorCode quirks

Postmark frequently returns **HTTP 422** with a JSON body that carries an `ErrorCode` field — the HTTP status alone is not enough to classify the failure.

| ErrorCode | Meaning | Maps to |
|---|---|---|
| `10` | Invalid API token | `PostmarkAuthError` (treated as 401) |
| `406` | Inactive recipient | `PostmarkInactiveRecipient` |
| Anything else with HTTP 422 | Bad request body | `PostmarkBadRequestError` |

### Retry behaviour (`helpers/utils.py::with_retry`)

| Status | Action |
|---|---|
| 400 / 401 / 403 / 404 / 409 / 422 (non-406) / 428 | Raise immediately |
| 422 + ErrorCode 406 | Raise `PostmarkInactiveRecipient` (caller decides) |
| 429 | Exponential backoff `base * 2 ** attempt + jitter`, capped at 32 s, up to 3 retries |
| 5xx | Same exponential backoff |
| `httpx.TimeoutException` / `httpx.NetworkError` | Wrapped as `PostmarkNetworkError`, retried |

### How errors surface to the gateway

`health_check()` catches typed exceptions and maps via `_STATUS_MAP`:

```
401 → ConnectorStatus(DEGRADED,  TOKEN_EXPIRED)
403 → ConnectorStatus(UNHEALTHY, INVALID_CREDENTIALS)
429 → ConnectorStatus(DEGRADED,  CONNECTED)   — logged as warning
transport → ConnectorStatus(OFFLINE, CONNECTED)
other → ConnectorStatus(DEGRADED, CONNECTED)
```

sections.title: Troubleshooting
sections.content:
| Symptom | Probable cause | Fix |
|---|---|---|
| `ConnectorStatus(OFFLINE, MISSING_CREDENTIALS)` at install | `server_token` blank, or Postmark returned 401 | Re-enter the server token in ACP. Postmark shows the token only once — rotate via Servers → API Tokens if lost. |
| `ConnectorStatus(UNHEALTHY, INVALID_CREDENTIALS)` at health_check | 403 from Postmark — token lacks scope | Open the server in Postmark → API Tokens → grant the missing scope and re-save. |
| `ConnectorStatus(OFFLINE, TOKEN_EXPIRED)` at health_check | 401 — server_token revoked | Rotate the token in Postmark and update the connector config. |
| `PostmarkAuthError: Account token required for this endpoint` | Called `list_servers` / `list_domains` / `get_server_by_id` without configuring `account_token` | Add `account_token` in ACP install fields (from Account → API Tokens). |
| `PostmarkInactiveRecipient` on `send_email` | Recipient was previously bounced or marked-spam and is now suppressed | Either pick a different recipient, OR call `activate_bounce(bounce_id)` for that recipient first (requires the bounce id from `list_bounces(inactive=True)`). |
| `PostmarkBadRequestError` HTTP 422 with `ErrorCode 300 "Invalid 'To'"` | Malformed recipient address | Validate the email syntax before calling `send_email`. |
| `postmark.retry` warnings spike with status=429 | Hitting Postmark's 600-req/min cap (or your `rate_limit_per_min` setting) | The client retries with exponential backoff. Lower `rate_limit_per_min` in install fields if another integration shares the token. |
| `send_email` raises `ValueError: send_email requires html_body or text_body` | Caller passed only `subject` | Always pass at least one body — Postmark rejects empty messages. |
| `send_email_with_template` raises `ValueError: requires exactly one of template_id or template_alias` | Caller passed both, or neither | Pick one selector. Aliases are recommended (stable across template rebuilds). |
| 422 from `send_email` mentioning a missing sender signature | `from_email` is not in a verified domain | Verify the domain in Postmark → Sender Signatures → Domains (DKIM CNAME + Return-Path TXT). |
| `ImportError: cannot import name 'PostmarkNetworkError'` | Older `exceptions.py` without alias | Already fixed — `PostmarkNetworkError = PostmarkServerError` alias preserved. |
